mentions.note: Traditional enterprise software facing pressure from AI-powered alternatives; pricing power under threat as customers explore cheaper custom AI solutions.
TurboTax and QuickBooks; down 45% at 19x earnings; AI threat minimal (consumer-facing); SMBs can't build internally—buyer right now.
Down 25% in January; very good company but enterprise software group hit by AI displacement fears; multiple compressed from 30x+ to mid-teens.
Down 11% on AI displacement fears. Good company but multiples compressing rapidly — don't chase this decline.
Down 35% Q1 but +8% since CEO Sasan Goodarzi spoke Feb 26; brand loyalty, consumer trust, expert network moat vs. AI replication; deserves comeback but needs time as market written it off.
Down 5% despite tax season (unusual); caught in AI/SaaS panic. Not expensive historically. Sellers aggressive—unclear why; sentiment shifted sharply.
Financial software down nearly 7% despite tax season strength; dragged down by software index rotation.
